mechanism_narrative: FEDFUNDS held near the zero lower bound (0.1%) reflects maximal monetary accommodation, which lowers the discount rate on future equity cash flows and incentivizes reallocation from cash/bonds into equities, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as long as the rate remains near zero.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or periods of near-zero FEDFUNDS show negative mean forward returns with statistical significance (p<0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE is elevated above 7% but has stopped rising or begun to decline month-over-month, it signals the trough of a labor market shock has passed, triggering a risk-on repricing that lifts S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as investors anticipate earnings recovery.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 7% AND UNRATE change over trailing 3 months <= 0
failure_condition: In triggered instances, forward 60d returns are negative or not significantly different from unconditional baseline (t-test p > 0.05) across a majority of historical episodes with UNRATE > 7%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in HOUST and RSAFS jointly indicate a broad-based rebound in both housing activity and consumer spending, a combination that signals resilient aggregate demand and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; neither series alone captures this demand-breadth signal.
failure_condition: Joint HOUST/RSAFS uptrend periods show forward 20d return hit rate below 50% or Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, indicating no incremental signal beyond either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime characterized by near-zero FEDFUNDS and depressed UNRATE stabilizing at high levels, a sustained rebound in INDPRO signals restoration of industrial capacity utilization and supply-side normalization, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter; this mechanism is regime-conditional because in a late-cycle expansion the same INDPRO rebound would instead signal overheating and monetary tightening risk, flipping the sign.
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with zero-bound monetary policy
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS near zero and UNRATE > 7%, INDPRO upturns fail to precede positive forward 60d SPX returns in more than half of episodes, or Rank IC is negative over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in PAYEMS reflect improving labor income generation capacity, which supports near-term consumer demand expectations and produces modestly positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week, functioning as a continuous always-on momentum signal regardless of regime.
failure_condition: Sign of monthly PAYEMS change fails to predict the sign of forward 5d SPX returns with hit rate above 52% over any rolling 3-year window, or Rank IC stays below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses below 0.25%, it indicates the central bank has hit the effective zero lower bound, an extraordinary accommodation regime historically associated with front-loaded risk-asset rallies as investors price in an extended period of cheap financing, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward 20d SPX returns during FEDFUNDS < 0.25% episodes are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or hit rate below 50% across historical zero-bound episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A positive GDPC1 print combined with rising RSAFS jointly confirms that both aggregate output and retail consumption are expanding together, a synchronized demand signal that predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter; GDPC1 alone is too lagged and low-frequency to time entries, but combined with the higher-frequency RSAFS confirmation the joint signal becomes actionable.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter change > 0 AND RSAFS 3-month change > 0
failure_condition: Joint GDPC1/RSAFS positive-growth episodes fail to show forward 60d SPX hit rate above 55%, or the interaction signal's Rank IC does not exceed the best single-series Rank IC by at least 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime where UNRATE remains extremely elevated (above 8%) but PAYEMS shows month-over-month gains, the labor market divergence signals an early inflection point that markets reward quickly, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next trading week; in a normal-cycle regime with UNRATE near structural levels, the same PAYEMS gain carries no comparable inflection signal and the effect vanishes.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes (UNRATE > 8% with rising PAYEMS) show forward 5d SPX hit rate at or below 50%, or returns are statistically indistinguishable from unconditional 5d return distribution (p > 0.05).
